content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 10, 2012


Greg Rolen, Esq.
General Counsel
Mt. Diablo Unified School District
1936 Carlotta Drive
Concord, California 94519

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I- 12-164

Dear Mr. Rolen:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the Mount Diablo Unified
School District (the "District") regarding the District's duties under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Commission does not provide formal
advice to third parties concerning another person's duties under the Act. (Regulation
18329(b)(8).) Because you have not named the official about whom you request advice, we
cannot provide formal advice. However, Regulation 18329(c)(1) does provide that informal
assistance may be requested by any person with a duty to advise other persons relating to their
duties or actions under the Act. As General Counsel of the District, you have such a duty and
therefore we are providing informal assistance. Informal assistance does not provide the
requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114;
Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

Please also note that there are other bodies of law, separate and apart from the Act's
conflict-of-interest provisions that may apply to your situation. Our advice is based solely on the
Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application of other incompatible activities and
conflict-of-interest laws that may apply including, but not limited to, common law conflict of
interest and Government Code Section 1090.

_________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. I-12-164
Page No. 2


QUESTION²

May a Board member submit an application for employment with the District while still sitting as a Board member?

CONCLUSION

The Act does not prohibit a Board member submitting an application for employment with the District while still sitting as a Board member. However, the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act limit the officials conduct in connection with the application as set forth below.

FACTS

You are the General Counsel at the Mt. Diablo Unified School District.

One of your District Board members announced that he would not be seeking reelection to the Board. The Board member then submitted an application for employment with the District. The Board member has no influence over the department for which he applied, except for normal Board oversight. The position has not yet been filled, and applications are still being accepted. The Board member will be off the Board before the position is offered.

ANALYSIS

Conflicts of Interest

The Act does not contain an outright prohibit on a Board member from applying for employment with his or her own agency while serving as a member of the Board. Government Code Section 1090 has such a prohibition under certain circumstances, but is not a section of the Act.

The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply to "public officials"³ (Sections 87100, 87103; Regulation 18700(b)(1)) making, participating in, or influencing governmental decisions. Your Board member will not be making or participating in the future hiring decision you describe, since he will have left office by that time.




_____________________

² You also asked whether the district was prohibited from hiring the Board member as a district employee. A conflict of interest under Section 87100, if one were to exist, is personal to the public official that has an economic interest in a decision and would not be imputed to other Board members or district staff deciding to hire or not hire a former Board member as a district employee.

³ A "public official" is "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government agency . . . ." (Section 82048.)

File No. I-12-164
Page No. 3

"Influencing" is broadly defined and also prohibits certain conduct when a conflict of
interest exists. "Influencing" is defined in Regulation 18702.3(a) to include any contacts,
appearances before, or other attempts to influence, any member, officer, employee or consultant
of the official's agency. However, Regulation 18702.4(b)(1) and (3) provide that an official is
not attempting to use his or her official position to influence a governmental decision before the
official's agency if the official:

• Appears in the same manner as any other member of the general public before an agency
in the course of its prescribed governmental function solely to represent himself or
herself on a matter which is related to his or her personal interests.

• Negotiates his or her compensation or the terms and conditions of his or her employment
or contract.

Revolving Door Prohibition

Finally, please be aware that some former local officials who leave governmental service
are subject to the Act's one-year ban for local officials in Section 87406.3. "Generally, this
restriction prohibits certain former local officials from communicating with their former
agencies, for compensation and in representation of another person, for the purpose of
influencing any legislative or administrative actions, including quasi-legislative and quasi-
judicial actions, or any discretionary actions involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or
revocation of a permit, license, grant or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property.
This section, however, would not bar future employment with the official's former agency. Since
your question does not implicate this provision we do not analyze it further.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:jgl

2 You also asked whether the district was prohibited from hiring the Board member as a district employee.

' A "public official" is "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government 
agency ... " (Section 82048.)
